episodes.title: "My Constituents Are Hurting"
episodes.description:
Pinellas County Commissioner Chris Latvala speaks candidly about the pain many Clearwater residents feel over the future of Downtown Clearwater and the concentration of property ownership in the hands of a single entity.

In this powerful exchange at the Pinellas County Commission meeting, Latvala says “my constituents are hurting” and explains why he supports exploring a long-term ground lease model to protect the public’s long-term interests while still encouraging redevelopment.

The discussion centers around the future of the county-owned downtown Clearwater properties, concerns about community control, and the opportunity to create a downtown vision that reflects the desires of Clearwater residents for generations to come.

This conversation is about more than development. It’s about who shapes the future of a city.

Learn more about the citizen-led vision for Downtown Clearwater at NewDowntown.org

#Clearwater #DowntownClearwater #PinellasCounty #ChrisLatvala #ClearwaterFlorida #Scientology #GroundLease #CommunityVision #LocalGovernment #CoachmanPark

episodes.title: What's your number? #SaveTheGarden
episodes.description:
Jill Gibbs delivers a direct challenge to the Clearwater City Council over the future of downtown Clearwater and the proposed vacation of public right-of-way.

In this speech, she contrasts leaders who listen to citizens with leaders who dismiss them, pointing to Miami Beach officials who placed voter approval protections on the ballot after hearing from residents. She asks a simple but powerful question to Clearwater’s elected officials:

“What number will finally convince you to do the will of your constituents?”

With nearly 9,000 petition signatures submitted, this moment could shape the future of downtown Clearwater for generations.

The Council will be discussing the #SaveTheGarden initiative on May 21, 2026 during a work session and will vote on the 23rd.

Learn more and get involved at SaveTheGarden.org

#Clearwater #DowntownClearwater #Scientology #PinellasCounty #CityCouncil #CitizenInitiative #LocalGovernment #savethegarden

episodes.title: Ask The Right Questions | #NewDowntown
episodes.description:
At a recent Pinellas County Commission meeting, Brooks Gibbs challenged leaders to slow down and make sure they’re asking the right questions before making a decision that will shape downtown Clearwater for generations.

Drawing from a story often attributed to Albert Einstein, he makes a simple but powerful point. If you spend all your time answering the wrong question, you can still end up with the wrong outcome. Right now, the county seems focused on how to generate enough money from these properties to pay down debt. But many citizens are asking something deeper. How do we make sure this land stays active, accessible, and in the hands of the public long term?

That’s where the conversation shifts. Residents are not pushing for a sale of public land. They’re advocating for a long-term ground lease. The difference matters. Selling creates a one-time payout and gives up control. A ground lease can still generate upfront money, but it also creates ongoing revenue and allows the county to maintain ownership and set the terms for how the land is used. One path relies on hope that things turn out well. The other builds in guarantees.

In this speech, Brooks walks through the legal realities behind deed restrictions and reversion clauses, explains why those tools are not as permanent as they sound, and challenges the idea that selling land is the safest or simplest option. He reframes the decision in a way that’s hard to ignore. Would you rather take millions today or build something that produces value for decades? Would you rather hope for the best or guarantee it?

At its core, this isn’t just about real estate. It’s about who controls the future of downtown Clearwater and whether that future is decided once or shaped over time.

Message the County Commissioners using our easy form at www.NewDowntown.org. Encourage them to choose a lease over a sale and preserve our downtown.

#newdowntown #LeaseTheLand

episodes.title: REJECTED by the Clerk! #savethegarden
episodes.description:
The City of Clearwater Just Rejected 75% of Our Petition. Here’s Why.

The City of Clearwater is doing everything it can to shut down the largest citizen-led petition initiative in the city’s history.

Over 8,000 citizens signed our petition to stop Scientology from taking public streets in downtown Clearwater without voter approval. The minimum number of signatures we needed to submit to get our new law on the next ballot was 7,100. But the City Clerk has now rejected the majority of those signatures—throwing out over 5,000 voices!

In this video, we break down:

Why the City Clerk rejected our petition
The technical arguments being used to silence voters
Why these claims don’t hold up legally
And what we are doing next to fight back

This is bigger than one street.
This is about your First Amendment right to petition your government.

We believe small technical errors should NOT silence thousands of citizens.

👉 Watch now to understand what’s really happening and how you can help.

Learn more:
🌐 https://SaveTheGarden.org

CALL TO ACTION: Contact our city council at www.SaveTheGarden.org and tell them to intervene and place our initiative on the ballot!

episodes.title: #SaveTheGarden Appreciation Event
episodes.description:
We had an EPIC celebration for all of our volunteers who walked miles, knocked on hundreds of doors, and collected thousands of signatures. We want to thank you for all of your hard work. We could not have done this #SaveTheGarden campaign without you.

This entire experience has unified our community and energized locals to get involved in our city's happenings. An engaged citizen is a hero in our eyes.

For all of those who came out to celebrate with us, THANK YOU! For all of those who signed... THANK YOU!

episodes.title: 8,000 Signatures Oppose Scientology Sale
episodes.description:
Thank you Ms. Barbara for your vision - and thank you Clearwater for signing our petition. We made sure your voice was heard.

Here is what happens next:
1. The City Clerk has 20 days to count, verify, and certify the signatures.

2. Once certified sufficient, the City Council must decide whether to adopt the ordinance themselves and make it law immediately.

3. If the council chooses not to adopt it, the measure would go on the August ballot for the voters of Clearwater to decide.

The eyes of Tampa Bay are on the City Council and staff to ensure that this gets placed on the August ballot.

SaveTheGarden.org

Credit: 10 Tampa Bay
Thanks for this coverage!

episodes.title: Scientology’s Lobbying Exposed
episodes.description:
In this episode, Brooks Gibbs breaks down the latest twist in the fight to save South Garden Avenue from being handed over to Scientology.

Save The Garden exists for one clear purpose: to stop private organizations—like Scientology—from quietly acquiring downtown public streets without voter approval. City Council can be influenced by political pressure and lobbying, but the people cannot.

This week’s discovery:
A Clearwater City Councilmember, David Albritton, was lobbied by Scientology and pushed into seeking legal advice from the Florida Attorney General—an office closely tied to Scientology donors.

Meanwhile, Councilmember Ryan Cotton—Albritton’s son-in-law—defended the move, despite clear concerns from the Mayor, Vice Mayor Lina Teixeira, and Councilman Mike Mannino.

This video highlights:
• How Scientology uses political donations and lobbying to gain influence
• Why South Garden Avenue is at the center of this battle
• The major conflict of interest on the City Council
• Why voter approval—not City Council—must decide the fate of public streets
• How Clearwater citizens can take back control through the petition initiative

Clearwater deserves transparency. Clearwater deserves accountability.
And Clearwater deserves the right to vote on public street giveaways.

👉 Sign the petition in person today.
Find your nearest signing location at https://SaveTheGarden.org

Let’s change the law and protect our downtown—together.

#SaveTheGarden #Clearwater #Scientology #LocalNews #CivicAction #FloridaPolitics #ProtectClearwater #BrooksGibbs #RightToPetition #GovernmentTransparency

episodes.title: Save The Garden | Episode 4: Project Normandy
episodes.description:
🚨 EPISODE 4: PROJECT NORMANDY — Scientology’s Secret Plot to Take Clearwater 🚨
👉 WATCH NOW. SHARE EVERYWHERE.
Visit https://SaveTheGarden.org
 to sign the petition.

For over 50 years, Clearwater’s struggle with Scientology has never been about religion — it’s been about real estate.

In this episode, Clearwater resident Brooks Gibbs exposes PROJECT NORMANDY — a covert operation personally written by L. Ron Hubbard to infiltrate, control, and ultimately own Clearwater, Florida. Hubbard’s secret memos detailed plans to “distinguish friends from enemies” and “handle as needed” in order to establish total area control.

When the FBI raided Scientology headquarters in 1977 — the largest raid in Bureau history — agents uncovered 48,000 pages of secret operations, blackmail plots, and land-grab strategies. Eleven Scientology leaders, including Hubbard’s wife, went to prison for espionage and conspiracy.

Yet the plan never stopped.
Today, Scientology and its shell companies own over 200 buildings across 100 acres in downtown Clearwater — worth over a billion dollars. Now, they’re trying to take our public streets, starting with South Garden Avenue.

💪 Save the Garden isn’t fighting against a religion — we’re standing up to a 50-year covert real estate scheme designed to control our city.

Thousands of Clearwater citizens have already signed our petition to stop this takeover. If you haven’t yet—please join us.
🖊️ One minute. One signature. One more shield for our streets.

But our movement faces pressure:
Fake names. Police calls. Investigations. Legal roadblocks.
All designed to silence ordinary citizens who simply want to protect public land.

Despite it all, our volunteers continue walking neighborhoods, talking with residents, and defending the people’s right to petition their government — a right enshrined in the Constitution.

📢 Together, we are reminding City Hall that government belongs to the people, not to a billion-dollar organization with a 50-year plan to dominate our town.

✅ TAKE ACTION:
1️⃣ Sign the petition at SaveTheGarden.org

2️⃣ Encourage 5 friends to sign too
3️⃣ Click “Message the Councilmembers” on our site and tell them: We stand with Save The Garden.

💚 Your voice matters. Your city matters.
#SaveTheGarden #ProjectNormandy #Clearwater #Scientology  #FBI #TrueCrime #ClearwaterFlorida

episodes.title: Podcast: Pastor Willy Rice Endorses #SaveTheGarden
episodes.description:
In this powerful episode, Pastor Willy Rice of Calvary Baptist Church in Clearwater shares his heartfelt endorsement of the #SaveTheGarden initiative. With clarity and conviction, Pastor Rice addresses the pressing decision facing the City Council: whether to sell a historic public street to the Organization of Scientology or preserve it for a public memorial garden honoring Clearwater’s original families and diverse heritage.

Pastor Willy offers a faith-driven, community-focused perspective on why this moment matters—not just for the city’s future, but for its soul. Tune in for an inspiring conversation about legacy, truth, and the power of public spaces to bring people together.

🔔 Don’t forget to like, comment, and subscribe to support this mission.
🌳 Learn more: SaveTheGarden.org
#SaveTheGarden #Clearwater #WillyRice #Scientology #FaithAndCivicDuty #BiblicalCitizenship
